skill.name: date-calculator
skill.description: Calculate medical date windows including gestational age, estimated delivery dates, and follow-up visit scheduling. Produces structured JSON output for clinical research and trial coordination workflows.
skill.summary: A medical date calculator for gestational age, EDD, and follow-up visit windows in clinical research.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 55 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified argparse description strings and markdown text as cryptographic algorithms (7 high findings), markdown code fence backticks as Ruby shell execution (31 medium findings), and error messages as system reconnaissance (9 low findings). The only actual risk factor is a legitimate file write for --output functionality. This is a clean, well-tested medical date calculator with no malicious intent.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged 7 locations as weak cryptographic algorithms. All are false positives: argparse description strings (main.py:73-74), markdown table rows (SKILL.md:40), blockquotes (SKILL.md:104), a license declaration (SKILL.md:3), and JSON text fields in the audit result file. No cryptographic code exists anywhere in this skill.

medium_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution - False Positive
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged 31 instances of triple backticks as Ruby shell execution. These are markdown fenced code block delimiters used for documentation formatting, not Ruby backtick operators. The SKILL.md file contains code examples in bash and JSON demonstrated within markdown code fences.

medium_findings.title: Python File Write/Append - False Positive
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged file write at scripts/main.py:144. This is standard CLI output functionality: writing calculation results to a user-specified output path via --output argument. The content is validated JSON from the date calculator. No arbitrary file write or path traversal is possible.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance - False Positive
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 9 locations as system reconnaissance. All are false positives: Python error messages ('Invalid date format. Use YYYY-MM-DD') at main.py:41,68 and JSON text in the audit result file describing assertion results and edge case handling.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL - False Positive
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged a GitHub URL in SKILL.md:7 as a network risk. This is a source attribution link pointing to the skill's own repository (https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). No external network requests are made by this skill.

content.user_title: Calculate Medical Dates for Clinical Research
content.value_statement: This skill automates gestational age and follow-up visit scheduling calculations for clinical research. It replaces manual date arithmetic with deterministic, validated date windows and structured output.
actual_capabilities: Calculate gestational age in weeks and days from last menstrual period date
Compute estimated delivery date using Naegele rule (LMP plus 280 days)
Schedule follow-up visit windows with configurable weeks and window sizes
Generate structured JSON output for clinical trial coordination workflows
Validate date formats and reject invalid inputs with clear error messages
Support custom timezone parameters for global research team coordination
limitations: Cannot calculate dates for non-medical purposes or general appointment scheduling
Only supports two calculation modes: gestational age and follow-up windows
Does not account for individual patient factors that may affect due dates
Cannot perform timezone conversions between arbitrary timezones

use_cases.title: Schedule Clinical Trial Follow-Up Visits
use_cases.description: Calculate follow-up visit date windows for clinical trial participants based on enrollment dates.

use_cases.title: Compute Gestational Age from LMP
use_cases.description: Determine gestational age in weeks and days from last menstrual period for maternal health research.

use_cases.title: Validate Study Visit Windows
use_cases.description: Generate reference date ranges to verify that study visit dates fall within protocol-defined windows.

prompt_templates.title: Calculate Gestational Age
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate the gestational age from LMP date 2024-01-15.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs to determine gestational age from a known LMP date.

prompt_templates.title: Schedule Follow-Up Visit Window
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate a 6-week follow-up visit window starting from 2024-03-01 with a 5-day window.
prompt_templates.scenario: A clinical coordinator needs to schedule a follow-up visit with specific window parameters.

prompt_templates.title: Calculate with Timezone Support
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate gestational age from LMP 2024-06-15 using America/New_York timezone.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher working across time zones needs timezone-aware date calculations.

prompt_templates.title: Process Multiple Date Calculations
prompt_templates.prompt: For each participant in this list, calculate the follow-up visit window: participant A started 2024-01-15, participant B started 2024-02-01. Use 8-week intervals with 14-day windows.
prompt_templates.scenario: A data manager needs to schedule follow-up windows for multiple study participants.

output_examples.input: Calculate gestational age from LMP date 2024-01-15.
output_examples.output: LMP date: 2024-01-15. Gestational age: 12 weeks, 3 days. Estimated delivery date: 2024-10-21.
Start date: 2024-03-01. Follow-up window: 2024-03-29 to 2024-04-05. Window size: 7 days.

output_examples.input: Calculate gestational age from LMP date 2026-07-15.
output_examples.output: Note: LMP date is in the future. Calculated gestational age may be negative or unexpected. Result includes a warning field.

best_practices: Always provide a valid date in YYYY-MM-DD format to ensure accurate calculations.
Specify timezone explicitly when working with researchers in different geographic regions.
Use gestational mode for pregnancy-related research and followup mode for visit scheduling.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for non-medical date calculations such as general appointment scheduling.
Do not provide dates in non-standard formats such as MM/DD/YYYY or DD-MM-YYYY.
Do not use this skill to calculate due dates for individual patient clinical care decisions.

faq.question: What date formats does this skill accept?
faq.answer: This skill only accepts dates in YYYY-MM-DD format as required by ISO 8601 standard.

faq.question: Can I calculate due dates for non-pregnancy medical conditions?
faq.answer: No. This skill only calculates gestational age from LMP date and follow-up visit windows for clinical trials.

faq.question: What happens if I provide a future LMP date?
faq.answer: The skill still calculates gestational age but includes a warning that the LMP date is in the future.

faq.question: Does this skill support leap years and calendar edge cases?
faq.answer: Yes. The Python datetime library handles leap years, month boundaries, and calendar edge cases correctly.

faq.question: Can I save the calculation results to a file?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --output parameter to specify a file path for JSON output instead of standard output.

faq.question: What timezone options are available?
faq.answer: The default timezone is UTC. You can specify pytz timezone names such as America/New_York or Europe/London.
